sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view collection-level query latency with Namespace Insights, you
must have [Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>),
[Project Observability Viewer](<Project Observability Viewer>),
[Project Data Access Read Only](<Project Data Access Read Only>),
[Project Data Access Read/Write](<Project Data Access Read/Write>), or
[Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) access to the project.

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
- The **Namespace Insights** tab is supported only for
  Atlas M10+ clusters.

- If you have [Queryable Encryption](manual/core/queryable-encryption) enabled for a collection, Namespace
  Insights doesn't display the collection in this location. Atlas
  redacts this information.

- Atlas displays top namespaces by default. Depending on the time
  range, a namespace might fall in and out of the monitored list.
  Atlas interpolates missing data when at least one host metric
  exists in both ends of the time range. If Atlas encounters
  missing data, dotted lines connect the available data points.

- If you reach the data limit for a page load, the following error
  message appears:

  ```
  Chart data limit reached. Try a smaller time window or fewer hosts.
  ```

- The **Namespace Insights** tab doesn't support
  [time series collections](manual/core/timeseries-collections).

Some high-latency [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) operations don't generate a slow
query log for the foreign collection. This can occur because slow query
logs correspond with operations that are reported in the
[Query Profiler](query-profiler), whereas latency metrics increment
only when a [collection lock](manual/faq/concurrency) is acquired.

If the `$lookup` query on a shard can perform a local read, the `$lookup`
doesn't record a separate operation for querying the foreign collection. A
local read refers to when the query on the foreign collection targets only the
same shard where the current operation is being executed. As a result, the
`$lookup` operation increases the Namespace Insights latency metrics and
operation counts, but does not generate a slow query log for the foreign
collection.

codeBlocks.content: Chart data limit reached. Try a smaller time window or fewer hosts.

sections.heading: Access Namespace Insights
sections.body: To access Namespace Insights:

sections.heading: Step 1: Go to .
sections.body:
- Click the name of the cluster to open the **Cluster** sidebar.

- Click **Query Insights** in the **Cluster** sidebar.

  The **Query Shapes** tab displays by default.

- Click the **Namespace** tab.

sections.heading: Filter Query Insights
sections.body:
You can filter the **Query Insights** page by node, node state
(primary or secondary), and shard. You can also filter by a custom date
range within the last seven days, or select one of the following preset
time ranges:

- 7 days

- 5 days

- 2 days

- 24 hours (default)

- 12 hours

- 8 hours

- 1 hour

When you apply a filter, Atlas re-fetches data for the charts and
table on the current screen.

> **Note:** [Analytics nodes](add-analytics-node) are included when you
> filter by secondary node state.

sections.heading: View Metrics in Charts
sections.body:
The **Namespace Insights** page displays two charts
that each visualize a selected metric for up to five selected namespaces over the filtered time range.

By default, Atlas charts metrics for pinned namespaces and top namespaces with the highest total latency.
To choose which namespaces to chart, see [Select Namespaces to Chart](select-namespaces-to-chart). To find and pin namespaces, see [Manage Pinned Namespaces](manage-pinned-namespaces).

To select which metrics to visualize and filter by operation type:

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the metrics to display.
sections.body:
Select one of the following metrics:

| Metric          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Total latency   | Rate that indicates the total combined server operation duration on a collection (total combined server operation duration / collection interval in seconds).                                    |
| Average latency | Difference between two server operation times, collected 10 minutes apart, divided by the difference between two total server operation count values collected over the same 10-minute interval. |
| P50 latency     | 50th percentile in the latency histogram.                                                                                                                                                        |
| P95 latency     | 95th percentile in the latency histogram.                                                                                                                                                        |
| P99 latency     | 99th percentile in the latency histogram.                                                                                                                                                        |
| Operation count | Rate that indicates the total number of server operations performed on a collection (total server operations / collection interval)                                                              |

sections.heading: Step 2: Select the operation type to display.
sections.body:
Select one of the following operation types:

- All operation types

- Read

- Write

- Command

sections.heading: Manage Pinned Namespaces
sections.body:
You can search and pin a maximum of five namespaces to the top of the
table from the **Pinned Namespace** dialog box or
**Search namespaces** search box.**Dialog-box:**Dialog BoxTo manage pinned namespaces from the **Pinned Namespace**
dialog box:

sections.heading: Step 1: Open the  dialog box.
sections.body:
- If you have no pinned namespaces, click the
  **Pin namespaces** button.

- If you have pinned namespaces, click the
  **Manage pinned namespaces** button.

The **Pinned Namespaces** dialog box displays.

sections.heading: Step 2: Search the namespaces.
sections.body:
Specify a namespace in the **Enter namespaces to pin**
search box. You can also pin planned namespaces that don't
exist yet.

sections.heading: Step 3: Pin the namespace.
sections.body: Click the namespace that you want to pin.

sections.heading: Step 4: (Optional) Unpin a namespace.
sections.body:
Click **Unpin** next to the namespace that you want
to unpin.**Search-box:**Search BoxTo manage pinned namespaces from the **Search namespaces** search box:

sections.heading: Step 1: Search the table.
sections.body:
Specify a namespace in the **Search namespaces** search
box.

sections.heading: Step 2: Pin the namespace.
sections.body:
- Click the ellipsis icon next to the namespace that
  you want to pin.

- Click **Pin**.

sections.heading: Step 3: (Optional) Unpin a namespace.
sections.body:
- Click the ellipsis icon next to the namespace that
  you want to unpin.

- Click **Unpin**.

sections.heading: Select Namespaces to Chart
sections.body:
Atlas displays up to five namespaces. It displays pinned namespaces
(if any) and then top namespaces with the highest total latency by
default. You can also manually select the namespaces to chart.

To select namespaces:

sections.heading: Step 1: Search the table.
sections.body:
Specify a namespace in the **Search namespaces** search
box.

sections.heading: Step 2: Select the namespace to display in the charts.
sections.body:
Click the checkbox next to the namespace.

> **Note:** You can select a maximum of five namespaces. If you have five
> namespaces and want to display another, you must deselect one.

sections.heading: Edit Columns
sections.body:
Atlas displays the following table columns by default for pinned
namespaces (if any) and top namespaces:

- Total latency for all operation types

- Average latency for all operation types

- P95 latency for all operation types

- Operation Count for all operation types

To adjust the metric and operation type columns:

sections.heading: Step 1: Expand the  drop-down.
sections.body: Click the **Edit Columns** button.

sections.heading: Step 2: Select the columns to display and hide.
sections.body:
- Expand the metrics to display or hide.

- Select the operation types to display.

- Deselect the operation types to hide.

sections.heading: Step 3: View the columns.
sections.body:
- Click inside the table to close the drop-down.

- Scroll to the right to view columns beyond the edge of the
  screen width.

sections.heading: Go to a Collection
sections.body:
You can go to a collection in the Atlas UI from the table.

To go to a collection:

sections.heading: Step 1: Search the table.
sections.body:
Specify a namespace in the **Search namespaces** search
box.

sections.heading: Step 2: Go to a collection.
sections.body:
- Click the ellipsis icon next to the namespace for
  the collection that you want to view.

- Click **Go to collection**.
